title: Bryson: Using 3D printer-made iron at Masters

content: Maxwell Hiller, the No. 2 offensive tackle and No. 7 overall recruit in the SC Next Junior 300, gave Florida and new head coach Jon Sumrall his verbal pledge Wednesday.

title: Rai 'wins,' young Fleetwood steals show at Par 3

content: There are no more friendlies or camps before the U.S. names its World Cup roster, so what does coach Mauricio Pochettino need to do before then?

content: Polling began at 7 a.m. and will conclude by 5 p.m. across 31,490 polling stations in 35 districts.

content: Over 2.7 crore voters are eligible to vote across the State, where the elections are being held in a single phase for all 140 constituencies, and 883 candidates are in the fray

content: The increase marks nearly a 60-fold jump, reflecting the growing use of the international financial services centre for structuring India-focused and global investment strategies

content: How empowered marginalised women are helping farmers increase their yield in this drought-prone region in Maharashtra. Scientists and farmers’ bodies validate their contribution for changing farmers’ lives

title: TMC government snatched Bengal people's right to land, gave those to Bangladeshi infiltrators: Nabin
content: Addressing an election rally in north Bengal's Alipurduar, he said once the BJP forms a "double-engine" government in the State, it will rectify the demographic alterations and throw out Bangladeshi infiltrators from the State

title: NCP-SP leader Samarjeetsinh Ghatge rejoins BJP
content: Samarjeetsinh Ghatge’s credentials are likely to help the BJP strengthen its wings in the sugar-belt districts of Pune, Kolhapur, Sangli, and Satara

title: Logic may not be the right tool to examine faith and belief systems, says Supreme Court
content: Nine-judge Bench headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant continues hearing on Sabarimala entry issue

title: Kharge expresses regret over ‘illiterate’ remark, says he respects people of Gujarat
content: Congress leader Mallikarjun Kharge apologises for calling Gujaratis ‘illiterate’, reaffirming his respect for the State’s people

title: Odisha Law Commission recommends a new law on hate speech, hate crime
content: It is recommended that person repeating crime related to hate speech and hate crime may have to undergo two to seven years of jail term with pecuniary fine of ₹50,000

title: IGoM reviews preparedness amid West Asia developments, ensures energy, food security
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh says government is ensuring uninterrupted availability of LPG, petrol, diesel and fertilizers, while maintaining supplies of essential commodities

title: States to be pushed for ‘utility led aggregation’ to meet PM Surya Ghar targets
content: Under this model, State electricity distribution companies will pay for installing rooftop solar systems in households that either can’t afford them or don’t have adequate infrastructure to instal rooftop systems

title: India-Turkiye talks hint at a thaw in bilateral relations
content: Talks follow consultations with Azerbaijan; indicate shift in New Delhi’s position on ties with countries supporting Pakistan

title: Humayun Kabir’s helicopter ride makes waves as queries emerge over its funding
content: “I have to address about 50 political rallies in the State and about 20 of them are with Owaisi saheb,” says the Aam Janata Unnyan Party founder and suspended Trinamool Congress leader

title: Sasmit Patra offers to quit as BJD Parliamentary Party leader in Rajya Sabha
content: While the immediate reason for Mr. Patra’s decision remains unclear, several BJD Rajya Sabha MPs are learnt to have favoured his removal from the leadership role

title: West Bengal Assembly elections: Promise of jobs remains big highlight of poll manifestos of Left Front and Congress
content: Left Front manifesto promises initiatives for heavy and medium scale industrialisation, setting up of new software and hardware parks; Congress manifesto looks like a copy of the Trinamool Congress poll promises

title: Poll-bound Assam trails in health and education indicators
content: Low basic vaccination, high maternal mortality, and increasing dropout rates at the higher education level are some of the challenges the State faces

title: Stage set for a keen contest, as Mamata files papers from Bhabanipur
content: The BJP’s candidate from Bhabanipur is the Leader of the Opposition in the West Bengal Assembly Suvendu Adhikari, who claims, ‘She and her party both will lose this time’

title: Edappadi K. Palaniswami, the lone star of AIADMK election campaign, steps up his pitch for power
content: The former Chief Minister is the main campaigner for the AIADMK across Tamil Nadu, with a stump speech focused on law and order issues, the State’s finances, concerns of government employees, drug menace, and rising inflation; he also highlights features of the manifesto of his party

title: Steps into past: 1,500-year-old reservoir unearthed on Elephanta island
content: Archaeological Survey of India excavation finds sophisticated stepped reservoir and proof of ancient maritime trade

title: Textbook row: academics ask President to intervene
content: By forcefully intervening in what is essentially an educational matter, threatening criminal contempt of court and banishing respected educationists without hearing them, the Supreme Court, had encroached upon academic freedom, the letter says

title: India welcomes Pakistan-brokered Iran-U.S. ceasefire, stresses dialogue and diplomacy
content: Government calls for de-escalation and unimpeded navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, does not acknowledge Pakistan’s role in U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks

title: Election Commission-Trinamool Congress meeting ends on an acrimonious note
content: Trinamool delegation says Chief Election Commissioner told them to ‘get lost’, poll body sources say the Commission asked Rajya Sabha Member Derek O’Brien not to shout and ‘maintain decorum’

title: Despite having nine large waterbodies, residents do not get clean groundwater
content: They say that if the government removes encroachments and brings excess water from Chembarambakkam to the lakes in the constituency, the drinking water problem can be solved; another issue is that stray animal population keeps rising though the authorities take steps to catch them

title: Every town to get basic sports infra in next three years, says A.P. Minister
content: Development work at Indira Gandhi Municipal Corporation Stadium is being taken at an initial estimated cost of around ₹170 crore, says Ramprasad Reddy.

title: Amaravati will be developed as global creative economy hub, says Minister
content: Kandula Durgesh discusses integrating content, culture, lifestyle and immersive design into tourism development with stakeholders at Hotel Investment Conference South Asia in Mumbai

title: Priyank Kharge directs zilla panchayat CEOs to ensure there is no drinking water shortage in Karnataka villages
content: As Karnataka is facing the peak summer heat, Minister for Rural Development and Panchayat Raj and IT and BT Priyank Kharge on Wednesday directed Chief Executive Officers of all zilla panchayats to take urgent and proactive measures to ensure that no village faces a shortage of drinking water during the summer months

title: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah accuses Election Commission of India of being ‘selective and complicit’
content: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah has accused the Election Commission of India of being ‘selective and complicit’ in its approach

title: All set for polling for Davangere South, Bagalkot byelections on April 9
content: A total of 34 candidates are in fray for the byelection including nine in Bagalkot and 25 in Davangere South constituency

title: EPS has no achievements to speak of during poll campaign, makes false accusations, says Stalin
content: The DMK president blamed the AIADMK leader for trying to scuttle Ungaludan Stalin initiative when it was introduced, by filing a petition in the court against it

title: Kerala Assembly polls: Two women athletes injured, one critical, after car carrying UDF workers hit them
content: The accident occurred on the Asramam Link Road shortly after the public campaign concluded at 6 p.m on Tuesday.

title: India to engage new government of Bangladesh constructively, to deepen ties, says Jaishankar
content: Relations will not be “held hostage” to one issue, say Bangladeshi sources privy to the discussion, referring to the issue of Sheikh Hasina’s presence in India

title: No devotee of Ayyappa would have moved SC challenging exclusion of women of menstruating age: Justice Nagarathna
content: The judge says the question whether a stranger to a denomination could question a practice in a public interest petition ought to be taken up as the first issue to be decided by the Sabarimala Bench

title: Special session on delimitation: T.N. CM Stalin says ‘DMK will not stand by and watch’
content: Stalin asked why there is a hurry to convene a special session of Parliament right in the middle of elections to five States

title: Cabinet clears draft amendment Bill to implement women’s quota
content: It proposes delimitation based on 2011 Census and expansion of Lok Sabha strength from 543 to 816 seats

title: PM Modi wants collective action on women’s reservation, wants all MPs to back amendment Bill
content: The Prime Minister says the 2029 Lok Sabha election and Assembly polls in the future must be held with provisions for women’s reservation

title: Pawan Khera accuses Assam CM Himanta of using the police to silence questions
content: In a recorded video message from an undisclosed location, the Congress leader says his party has only sought answers and would continue to do so

title: Struggling With Loans? A Step-By-Step Guide On How To Secure 750+ CIBIL Score
content: A high score signals lower risk to lenders, while a low score suggests a borrower may struggle to meet repayment obligations.

title: In 1st Polls After Delimitation, BJP Hopes For Third Straight Term In Assam: 10 Points
content: The battle for 126 seats in Assam comes amid a bitter political row between the ruling BJP and the Opposition Congress over the corruption allegations against Chief Minister Himanta Sarma. The BJP is expecting a third straight term in the state.

title: 'Ketamine Queen' Jailed For 15 Years For Selling Drugs To Matthew Perry
content: Jasveen Sangha, 42, was one of five people charged over the death of the beloved Canadian-American actor.

title: TVK To Play Spoiler For Congress-DMK As Puducherry Votes Today? 10 Points
content: The NDA is hoping for a second straight term in Puducherry, where 30 seats are up for election today. The pitch, though, has been queered by the entry of the fledgling TVK, which has set up multi-cornered contests in many seats.

title: Congress Waits In The Wings As CPM Crosses Fingers For A Third Term In Kerala
content: The election for 140 assembly seats in Kerala today is set to be a riveting contest for a state that threw a surprise in the last election. There is speculation that today's single-phase election could be more unpredictable than the one in 2021.

title: Iran Closes Hormuz After Israel Strikes Lebanon With 100 Missiles In 10 Minutes
content: US Iran War US Iran Ceasefire Donald Trump Iran Closes Strait Of Hormuz In Response To Israeli Strikes On Lebanon

title: "Political Disaster": Israel Opposition Blasts Netanyahu Over Iran Ceasefire
content: The United States and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire in an 11th-hour bid to avert all-out destruction of Iran threatened by US President Donald Trump.

title: India Enters New Nuclear Era As Kalpakkam Reactor Attains Criticality
content: With this achievement, the country formally entered the second stage of the three-stage nuclear power programme envisioned by Homi J Bhabha.

title: Iran Announces Alternative Routes In Hormuz Strait
content: Iran announced alternative routes on Thursday for ships travelling through the Strait of Hormuz, citing the risk of sea mines in the main zone of the vital waterway.

title: North Korea Says Latest Tests Used Cluster-Bomb Warhead Missiles
content: The report by North Korean state media came a day after South Korea's military said it detected North Korea firing multiple missiles from an eastern coastal area in its second round of launches in two days.

title: "Impressive": Nuke Watchdog Praises India's Nuclear Milestone At Kalpakkam
content: The landmark step marks a significant advancement in the country's indigenous nuclear programme and its pursuit of long-term energy sustainability.

title: Trump Meets NATO Chief Amid Talk Of Pulling Out Of Military Alliance
content: Trump has been a longtime critic of NATO and in his first term had suggested he had the authority on his own to leave the alliance.

title: Shaky US-Iran Ceasefire In Doubt As Israeli Strikes Kill Over 180 In Lebanon
content: Iran War: Lebanon said at least 182 people were killed and hundreds were wounded, making it the deadliest day in the latest Israel-Hezbollah war.

title: JD Vance To Lead 1st Round Of US-Iran Talks In Pakistan: White House
content: US President Donald Trump is "dispatching his negotiating team", led by the Vice President of the United States, to Islamabad for talks this weekend.

title: Choose Truce Or "Continue War Via Israel": Iran Warns US Over Lebanon Attacks
content: White House has dismissed the inclusion of Lebanon in the temporary ceasefire agreement, backing Israel's continued military operation against Hezbollah.

title: "Who's This Segal?": Supreme Court On Centre's Objection To DY Chandrachud's View On Adultery
content: Citing foreign articles and judgments quoted by former Chief Justice DY Chandrachud in his verdict decriminalising adultery, the Centre said that "blanket reliance on international judgments is impermissible"

title: Iran Accuses US Of Violating Key Clauses Of Peace Framework Before Talks
content: A fresh diplomatic row has erupted after Iranian officials accused the United States of violating three clauses of the 10-Point Proposal, the agreed framework for upcoming negotiations.

title: DC vs GT: What was David Miller thinking — why didn’t he run?
content: David Miller's decision to refuse a single in the final over of the IPL 2026 match against Gujarat Titans cost Delhi Capitals a potential win. Despite an injured hand, Miller's late surge brought them close, but his final act left fans questioning his judgment as they lost by one run.

title: 'Interesting thought': Raghav Chadha hints at own political party
content: Sidelined AAP leader Raghav Chadha has fueled speculation about his political future after responding to a social media video suggesting he launch his own party. This comes after the AAP removed him as deputy leader in the Rajya Sabha, with Chadha stating he was "silenced, not defeated."

title: Lowe CEO Marvin Ellison has a 'reality check' for Silicon Valley spending billions on AI
content: Lowe's CEO Marvin Ellison issued a stark warning about AI's limitations, emphasizing that while it can handle administrative tasks, it cannot perform essential skilled trades. He announced a $250 million investment over 10 years to train 250,000 skilled tradespeople, aiming to bolster the American blue-collar workforce.

title: Crude prices climb back towards $97 as Strait of Hormuz remains under pressure
content: Oil prices edged higher Thursday after a significant daily drop, as Middle East tensions and uncertainty surrounding the Strait of Hormuz kept markets on edge. Despite conflicting reports on tanker movement, US Vice President Vance indicated the strait was beginning to reopen. Prices remain elevated due to persistent concerns over potential renewed hostilities.

title: Anthropic CEO says AI will take your job;but his own company find enough people to work
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei is warning that AI could eliminate more than 50% of entry-level white-collar jobs within five years. But inside his own company, the picture looks different. Claude Code creator Boris Cherny says the software engineer job title is already dying — and Anthropic's Head of Growth says they can't hire enough PMs to keep up. The disruption isn't coming. It's already here.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection Day 21: Ranveer Singh starrer dips under Rs 10 crore
content: Aditya Dhar's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its record-breaking spree, crossing Rs 1,000 crore net in India and achieving a worldwide gross of Rs 1,653.67 crore. Despite a slight dip on its 21st day, the film has secured a spot in the top 10 highest-grossing films globally for 2026, marking a significant international triumph for Indian cinema.

title: Netanyahu warns 'finger on trigger', calls ceasefire ‘not the end’
content: Israel's Prime Minister Netanyahu declared a fragile two-week ceasefire with Iran, emphasizing readiness to resume military action if objectives aren't met. Despite cooperation with the US-Iran truce, Israel will continue striking Hezbollah. Meanwhile, 254 deaths were reported in Lebanon from Israeli strikes.

title: Anthropic withholds Mythos AI model after tests by JPMorgan, Apple, Google and more
content: AI firm Anthropic has developed a powerful tool, Claude Mythos Preview, capable of finding complex software flaws. Due to its potential misuse, the company is withholding public access, instead partnering with tech giants like Apple, Google, and Microsoft through Project Glasswing. This initiative aims to leverage the AI's capabilities to identify and patch critical vulnerabilities, bolstering global cybersecurity efforts.

title: Candidates: How R Vaishali crushed Divya Deshmukh in 31 moves to join lead
content: Indian chess sensation R Vaishali secured a commanding victory over compatriot Divya Deshmukh at the Women's Candidates, seizing a co-lead. Meanwhile, R Praggnanandhaa faced another draw, struggling to convert his advantage against Wei Yi. In the Open section, Javokhir Sindarov leads the pack with 7/9 points. The tournament continues with crucial pairings in both sections.

title: VP Vance will lead US team for truce talks with Iran in Pakistan: White House
content: Vice President JD Vance will lead a US delegation to Pakistan this weekend for mediated talks with Iran, aiming to secure a permanent ceasefire. The discussions in Islamabad follow a two-week truce, with the White House emphasizing the need for the Strait of Hormuz to remain open. Lebanon is not included in the current ceasefire agreement.

title: Serena Williams shares rare Easter pics, opens up on motherhood journey
content: Serena Williams shared rare Easter photos with Alexis Ohanian and daughters Olympia and Adira, calling family time everything. She also spoke on Today about motherhood, her daughters’ personalities, and learning to balance attention between them. Her words and photos showed a simple, honest side of life after tennis, focused fully on family and everyday moments.

title: Megan Thee Stallion recalls emotional moment amid Klay Thompson romance
content: The Dallas Mavericks’ star player, Klay Thompson, has proven his love and loyalty towards the popular Grammy winner, Megan Thee Stallion, for close to a year of their high profile romance. While Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson seem happy together, the popular artist had gone through a lot in her life, including witnessing her mother’s brutal death in 2019.

title: Mike Vrabel & Dianna Russini Drama Intensifies Over Scot McCloughan Affair
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and Dianna Russini of The New York Times have found themselves in major trouble after photos of them at an upscale hotel sparked outrage and taken the world by storm. The two were spotted relaxing together and sharing a hug which has convinced fans that they are having an alleged affair. Though both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have denied such rumors, an alleged old affair of hers has resurfaced.

title: After SIR, number of voters in Mamata Banerjee's seat down 25%
content: Bhowanipore constituency, the high-profile seat of Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee, has seen its electorate shrink by nearly 25% after electoral roll revisions. Over 51,000 deletions occurred, with a significant portion of Muslims marked for adjudication and eventual deletion, raising concerns among researchers.

title: IPL 2026: Jos Buttler scripts history, enters elite six-hitting club
content: Jos Buttler achieved a significant milestone, hitting his 600th six in T20 cricket. He joined an elite group of players with this achievement. Gujarat Titans secured a thrilling one-run victory over Delhi Capitals. KL Rahul's strong performance for Delhi was not enough to secure the win. The match saw impressive batting displays from both sides.

title: Tamil Nadu chief secretary, DGP shifted; Stalin cries foul
content: The Election Commission replaced Tamil Nadu's Chief Secretary and DGP ahead of polls, citing complaints of administrative bias towards the ruling DMK. Chief Minister M.K. Stalin vehemently criticized the EC, accusing it of acting as a BJP 'branch' and attempting to hinder DMK's victory. He questioned the sudden change in assessment of the state's administration.

title: Delhi records coolest April day in 10 years
content: Delhi experienced its coolest April day in a decade, with temperatures plummeting to 28.2°C due to light rain and overcast skies. While a welcome respite, temperatures are set to climb significantly, potentially reaching 38°C by April 14. The recent rainfall also improved air quality, though a dip back to moderate is expected.

title: Sabarimala PIL was entertained in 2006, but would have been dismissed now: SC
content: The Supreme Court expressed concern over the misuse of Public Interest Litigation for personal agendas, suggesting it would have dismissed a PIL challenging the Sabarimala custom. Justices noted that such challenges, if not brought by devotees, lack cause of action. The court emphasized the need for strict locus standi rules to prevent PIL abuse, especially in religious matters.

title: ‘You can’t talk like this,’ observer shoots back at CEC Gyanesh Kumar, gets removed
content: A heated exchange erupted during a high-level election review meeting when a senior observer challenged the Chief Election Commissioner. The observer was subsequently removed, with the EC citing professional incompetence for failing to provide basic details about polling booths. The meeting also addressed vulnerable booths and assured facilities at polling stations.

title: Woman murdered, body found inside bed box in Delhi; 3 including lover arrested
content: A married woman with three children was found murdered and hidden in a storage box at a Delhi PG accommodation. Police arrested her alleged lover, Deepak, and two relatives, suspecting a dispute escalated to violence. Evidence suggests accomplices helped conceal the crime. The family had warned her about the relationship.

title: Appointment of priests by govt boards can't be a secular act: Centre
content: Solicitor General Tushar Mehta criticized the Supreme Court's past rulings on temple priest appointments, arguing they wrongly classified a religious decision as secular. He urged a review of jurisprudence, emphasizing state non-interference in religious matters and advocating for legislative control over social and religious reforms.

title: Sanitation workers appointed as BLOs for pre-SIR exercise in Hyderabad
content: Hyderabad's old city residents seeking voter list assistance encountered sanitation workers instead of trained officials. These workers, appointed due to staff shortages, lacked the necessary skills for the pre-SIR mapping exercise. They were equipped with smartphones to record voter details. Officials stated this is a temporary measure and replacements are expected soon.

title: West Bengal polls: No Muslim name finds place in BJP's list
content: The BJP has not nominated any Muslim candidates in the Bengal elections, a departure from previous polls despite a significant Muslim membership. Party leaders cited 'winnability' as the primary criterion for candidate selection, suggesting a disconnect between the BJP's platform and Muslim voting patterns. In contrast, Congress, TMC, and the Left Front have fielded a considerable number of Muslim candidates.

title: Pawan Khera seeks bail from Telangana high court in Himanta Sarma case
content: Congress leader Pawan Khera has sought anticipatory bail from the Telangana High Court. This follows a case filed by Guwahati police concerning his allegations against Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife. Khera claims the FIR copy hasn't been provided and the charges are invalid. Assam Police's pursuit has now extended to Hyderabad.

title: Cut airport fees for domestic flights by 25% for 3 months: Government
content: Aviation ministry is providing relief to airlines. Major airports will cut landing and parking charges for domestic flights by 25 percent. This reduction is for three months. The move aims to ease financial pressure on airlines. It comes amid rising fuel costs and airspace restrictions. This decision is effective immediately.

title: Government clears bills to amend quota law for women
content: The government has approved draft bills to amend the women's reservation law, aiming for 33% reservation in Lok Sabha and state assemblies from 2029. These bills, to be introduced in the upcoming special Parliament session, will increase Lok Sabha seats to 816, with 273 reserved for women.

title: India welcomes Pak-brokered ceasefire, hopes it can lead to peace in Ukraine too
content: India has welcomed a temporary ceasefire in West Asia. The truce, brokered by Pakistan, aims to end a brutal war. India hopes this will lead to lasting peace and ensure global trade flows freely. The government also sees potential for peace efforts in Ukraine. India prioritizes the safety of its diaspora and LPG tanker passage.

title: Taylor Swift’s wedding plans take hit as she faces a major setback while Travis Kelce remains distant
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are just days away from tying the knot after being together for over two years as they flaunt their love and commitment to billions of fans worldwide. While the high profile couple continue to maintain strict privacy around their high profile wedding, the billionaire pop star, Taylor Swift, has faced a massive setback in her life.

title: China played bigger role than Pak in getting Iran to negotiation table
content: China has confirmed its "own efforts" to broker a ceasefire between the US and Iran, a move echoed by President Trump who credited Beijing with bringing Tehran to the negotiating table. Following a two-week pause in hostilities, diplomatic outreach by China, including extensive talks and a peace initiative with Pakistan, aims for long-term regional stability.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for April 9
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for April 9, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: US desperate for ceasefire? Trump forced Pakistan to broker temporary truce with Iran
content: For weeks, the Trump administration relied on Pakistan to act as an intermediary, urging it to persuade Iran to agree to a pause in hostilities that would allow the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Islamabad’s role as a Muslim-majority neighbour was seen as key to making the proposal acceptable to Tehran. Back-channel diplomacy spearheaded by Pakistan’s army chief Asim Munir culminated late Tuesday with the US, Iran and Israel announcing a two-week ceasefire.

title: Strait of Hormuz: How Trump gave Iran a weapon deadlier than any nuke
content: A recent conflict has shifted control of the vital Strait of Hormuz. Iran may now charge transit fees for ships passing through. This follows a US-Iran ceasefire agreement.  The situation signals a significant change in regional maritime control.  Global oil trade routes are impacted by these developments.

title: Los Angeles Dodgers legend Davey Lopes passes away at 80 after Parkinson’s disease fight
content: Davey Lopes, a former Los Angeles Dodgers star, died at 80 after battling Parkinson’s disease, according to TMZ Sports. He won two World Series titles, earned four All-Star selections, and recorded 557 stolen bases. Lopes later became a coach and manager, working with several MLB teams. His impact on baseball continues to be remembered by fans and players alike.

title: 'It doesn't matter who you are': Sooryavanshi steals fresh IPL buzz
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is emerging as a standout performer in IPL 2026, impressing with fearless batting against top bowlers like Jasprit Bumrah. Dale Steyn said the youngster has already “instilled fear” in bowlers. His quick 39 helped Rajasthan Royals beat Mumbai Indians, extending their winning run, with his aggressive approach troubling even the best in the game.

title: From Brahmaputra to backwaters - Assam, Kerala, Puducherry head to vote
content: Voters in Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry are heading to the polls today to decide the fate of numerous candidates. Assam witnesses a direct fight between the BJP-led NDA and Congress. Kerala faces a choice between continuity with the ruling LDF and a comeback bid by the UDF. Puducherry presents a multi-cornered contest with new political forces emerging.

title: BJK Cup: Vaishnavi, Sahaja post milestone wins; India beat New Zealand
content: Vaishnavi Adkar and Sahaja Yamalapalli earned their first wins in India colours, helping India take an unbeatable lead against New Zealand in the BJK Cup Asia/Oceania Group 1 tie. Later, Ankita Raina and Rutuja Bhosale ensured a clean 3-0 win.

content: los Angeles Lakers were 30-1 for the title, but a major injury to Luka Doncic lengthened them to 100-1 . the lakers are now 300-1 to win the championship this season . los Angeles continues to approach odds-on title status, moving from +135 to +120 .

title: Women's transfer rankings for 2026-27 season
